content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 5, 1985


Pat Miranda
Mayor
City of Irwindale
5050 North Irwindale Avenue
Irwindale, CA  91706

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-85-248

Dear Mayor Miranda:

     This is in response to your letter dated November 26, 1985
in which you sought the Commission's advice regarding a
potential conflict of interest under Section 1090 of the
Government Code.

     Although the Commission is authorized to render advice
regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015), we have not been
authorized to give advice regarding other conflict of interest
laws such as Government Code Section 1090. The Commission
realizes that it would be more convenient for public officials,
such as yourself, to have all of their conflict of interest
questions answered by one agency, and the Commission has
directed its staff to explore possible alternatives to bring
this about. However, at this time no concrete proposal has
been formulated.

     Therefore, lacking any authority to render advice regarding
Section 1090, we must decline your request and instead
recommend that you contact the Attorney General's Office for
such advice. The Commission cannot request an opinion from the
Attorney General on your behalf; however, your representatives
in the Legislature may do so.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Pat Miranda
December 5, 1985
Page 2


If we can ever be of assistance to you regarding your
responsibilities under the Political Reform Act, please do not
hesitate to contact us again.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:JG:plh

November 26, 1985

Fair Political Practices
Commission
Suite 800
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Mr. Leydeigh

Dear Mr. Leydeigh:

My name is Pat Miranda, and I am the Mayor of the City of
Irwindale and Chairman of its Community Redevelopment Agency.

Referring to the diagram above, I own Parcel 1; another council-
man owns Parcel 2; still another councilman owns Parcel 5.
Parcels 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 are zoned commercial; Parcel 5 is
zoned residential.

The other two councilmen own property within one-half mile of
this site as do 90% of the population of this City of 1,000 resi-
dents. Parcel 3 is owned by the C.R.A., and one-half of Parcel 4
is owned by the owner of Parcel 5.

Fair Political Practices
Commission
Mr. Leydeigh
November 26, 1985
-2-


The C.R.A. wishes to sell Parcel 3 to a developer.  The developer
wants Parcels 1 and 2 and perhaps Parcel 4 as well.  I am not
worried about FPPC because I will abstain on all votes and will
not otherwise participate.  After Call v Thompson, I am worried
about 1090 questions.  If the developer buys my land to make a
better development, how can I get an answer to my worries about
1090? Is there any way you can get an answer from the Attorney
General?

Very truly yours,

CITY OF IRWINDALE

Pat Miranda, Mayor

PM/ap
